MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-20-2016 Set For Trial On Dec-05-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT On Plaintiffs Santos Rafael Puc May And Jose Delfino Pucs Operative Complaint By Defendants Adib Khouri, Antelope Halawan Properties, Llc, Faiq And Diana Khouri Living Trust, Faiq I. Khouri And Diana F. Khouri, As Individuals And As Trustees Of The Faiq And Diana Khouri Living Trust
Off Calendar Nov-03-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS ADIB KHOURI, ANTELOPE HALAWAN PROPERTIES, LLC, FAIQ AND DIANA KHOURI LIVING TRUST, FAIQ KHOURI AND DIANA KHOURI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT On Plaintiffs Santos Rafael Puc May And Jose Delfino Pucs Operative Complaint- Defendants' motion for summary judgment is granted. (Saelzler v. Advanced Group 400 (2001) 25 Cal.4th 763; Leslie G v. Perry & Assocs. (1996) 43 Cal.App.4th 472.) Plaintiffs allege that defendants breached their duty of care and caused the death of Jose Manuel Puc May by maintaining a dilapidated apartment house with lax security that was overrun with criminals. While it can be reasonably inferred that decedent was attacked in defendants' building, plaintiffs cannot establish that defendants' breach of duty caused the death of Mr. May. Neither plaintiffs nor their expert has any information about who committed the crime, a motive for the crime, or how the assailant(s) entered into the building. (Defendants' Undisputed Material Facts 8-10.). Defendants' UMF 8 indicates that the assailants may have resided in the building. If the assailants resided in the building, then they were entitled to be on the premises and whether non-tenants or vagrants had access to the building is irrelevant. Defendants' evidence shifts the burden of production to plaintiff. (Leslie G at 482.) Plaintiffs fail to create a triable issue whether defendants' lack of security measures and/or failure to maintain the premises caused Mr. May's death. The authorities relied on by plaintiffs are distinguishable. In Vasquez v. Residential Investments, Inc. (2004) 118 Cal.App.4th 269, the court denied the landlord's motion for summary judgment based on causation because there was a triable issue whether the landlord's failure to replace a missing pane of glass in the apartment's front door that the murderer used to gain entry was a substantial factor in causing the attack. In Vasquez, the murderer was caught and testified that he relied on the broken pane to obtain entry. In Raven H. v. Gammette (2007) 157 Cal.App.4th 1017, the assailant that raped the plaintiff tenant broke into her apartment through an unsecured window. The court denied summary judgment and found that there was a triable whether the landlord's failure to put bars on her window, even though bars were on other tenants windows, was a substantial factor in causing the crime. In contrast, plaintiffs present no evidence that a condition of the property or lax security was a substantial factor in causing the injury. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Kelley De Young, CSR #6780 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Feb-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ADIB KHOURI, ANTELOPE HALAWAN PROPERTIES, LLC, FAIQ AND DIANA KHOURI LIVING TRUST, FAIQ KHOURI AND DIANA KHOURI'S Motion To Strike the punitive damages allegations is granted. Punitive damage may not be recovered in a wrongful death action. (Ford Motor Co. v. Superior Court (1981) 120 Cal.App.3d 748, 751). ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ADIB KHOURI, ANTELOPE HALAWAN PROPERTIES, LLC, FAIQ AND DIANA KHOURI LIVING TRUST, FAIQ KHOURI AND DIANA KHOURI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled. Plaintiffs' claim for wrongful death is not necessarily barred by res judicata. The order sustaining the demurrer without leave to amend in the prior case recites that no opposition was filed. The court adopts the reasoning of Judge Curiel in Ros v. U.S. Bank, National Association (S.D. CA 2013) 2013 Sl WL 3190684 at *5 that, in the absence of any evidence that the prior order was based on the court's review of the merits of the positions advanced by the demurring party rather than simply the lack of any opposition to the demurrer, res judicata doesn't apply. It is highly unlikely that Judge Goldsmith did such a substantive review and defendants have provided no evidence that he did. Defendants' request to take judicial notice of Exhibits A-E is granted. Prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
